introSection:
Dragon Fyre is a steel roller coaster manufactured by Arrow HUSS that is located at Canada's Wonderland in Vaughan, Ontario, Canada.

The coaster is a mirror layout of Big Loop at Heide Park.

sections.title: History
sections.content:
The roller coaster is one of four which opened with Canada's Wonderland in 1981. It was named "Dragon Fyre" until it was changed to Dragon Fire for the 1997 season. For the 2019 season, several other attractions in the Medieval Faire area had their original names restored, including Dragon Fire.

Leviathan, opened in 2012, passes over Dragon Fyre. At a height of, Leviathan's first drop is taller than Dragon Fyre.

sections.title: Design
sections.content: Since the removal of Drachen Fire from Busch Gardens Williamsburg, Dragon Fyre is the only Arrow HUSS coaster in existence to have counter-clockwise turning corkscrews.

sections.title: Colour scheme
sections.content: Green track with yellow rails and purple supports. The supports used to be brown, but where repainted in 2005.

sections.title: Trains
sections.content: Dragon Fyre originally had three trains. For the 1987 season, one was transferred to the newly built Bat, a Vekoma Boomerang which uses the same track system as Dragon Fyre.

fullText:
Dragon Fyre is a steel roller coaster manufactured by Arrow HUSS that is located at Canada's Wonderland in Vaughan, Ontario, Canada.

The coaster is a mirror layout of Big Loop at Heide Park.

The roller coaster is one of four which opened with Canada's Wonderland in 1981. It was named "Dragon Fyre" until it was changed to Dragon Fire for the 1997 season. For the 2019 season, several other attractions in the Medieval Faire area had their original names restored, including Dragon Fire.

Leviathan, opened in 2012, passes over Dragon Fyre. At a height of, Leviathan's first drop is taller than Dragon Fyre.

Since the removal of Drachen Fire from Busch Gardens Williamsburg, Dragon Fyre is the only Arrow HUSS coaster in existence to have counter-clockwise turning corkscrews.

Green track with yellow rails and purple supports. The supports used to be brown, but where repainted in 2005.

Dragon Fyre originally had three trains. For the 1987 season, one was transferred to the newly built Bat, a Vekoma Boomerang which uses the same track system as Dragon Fyre.
